topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone screen, a familiar chime that usually signaled a text from a friend, but this time it was different. It was a direct message from Mr. Harrison. My heart gave a sudden, erratic thump against my ribs. I froze, my hand hovering over the screen, the glow illuminating the slight tremor in my fingers. He had tagged me in a photo from the school event, a candid shot I hadn't even realized he'd taken. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, making my breath catch in my throat. I quickly minimized the app, my eyes darting towards the classroom door, half-expecting him to materialize there. The silence in my room felt heavy, suffocating.

[story 2]

I was scrolling through my feed, mindlessly flicking through posts, when I saw it. A picture of me, taken moments before I walked into the library, captioned with a cryptic comment only I would understand. A wave of intense unease washed over me, making my palms slick. I stared at the image, my jaw tightening so hard I thought my teeth might crack. Who would be posting this? My gaze flicked towards my desk, searching for any sign of surveillance, any hidden camera. The air in the quiet study hall seemed suddenly thin, and I felt an overwhelming urge to bolt, to disappear into the shadows.

[story 3]

It happened during the late afternoon lull. I was reviewing notes when my phone buzzed. It was a direct message from Professor Evans. The message was simple: a link to a private group chat. My stomach lurched; I felt a sudden, sharp need to check the message again, to see if it was a mistake. My fingers trembled as I tapped the link. As the chat opened, I saw his profile picture—the same one from the lecture hall—and a recent post that seemed oddly timed. A deep chill traced its way down my spine. I slowly lowered the phone, my gaze fixed on the blank wall opposite me, my breathing becoming shallow and quick.
